overview: This PR improves auth error handling by replacing silent None returns with explicit exceptions, and updates the middleware to properly propagate error messages to clients.

threads.title: Replace silent auth failures with explicit exceptions
threads.summary: The token validator previously returned None on failure, losing context about why authentication failed. This thread adds input validation and converts jwt errors into typed AuthError exceptions.
threads.root_cause: Silent None returns on auth failure made debugging impossible and prevented the middleware from returning useful error messages.

steps.narration: Add a guard clause to TokenValidator.__init__ so that misconfiguration is caught at startup rather than producing cryptic errors at request time.
Convert jwt decode exceptions into typed AuthError exceptions, preserving the specific failure reason (expired vs invalid) instead of collapsing both into None.

threads.title: Update middleware to handle auth exceptions
threads.summary: With TokenValidator now raising exceptions instead of returning None, the middleware needs to catch these errors and translate them into proper HTTP responses with error details.
threads.root_cause: The middleware was checking for None returns which no longer happens — it needs to catch AuthError exceptions instead.

steps.narration: Wrap TokenValidator construction in a try/except to translate the new ValueError into a ConfigError, and update request handling to catch AuthError exceptions instead of checking for None.
